result.lens: Head-on width-2 interface classification (series-parallel port calculus; parallel composition as the unique birth channel for forcing)
result.first_principles:
Start from the detectors: a chi>=6 proof must consume objects that exist only in R^2. Three entry points. (1) Raw material: any clamp is itself a chi=5 graph, and no chi>=3 finite graph embeds in Q^2 (chi=2), no chi>=5 in the L^infty plane (chi=4) or R^1 (chi=2). (2) Width-2 rigidity: two unit circles meet in at most 2 points, so a 2-vertex port pins gluings to discrete choices; L^infty unit circles are squares meeting in continua. (3) The lift from one forced pair to a forbidden distance needs O(2)-transitivity on equal-length pairs (the L24 Lean-checked lift); L^infty isometries preserve only 8 directions.

The algebra: a width-2 edge port at k=5 has 20 states (ordered distinct color pairs); S_5 splits pairs-of-states into 7 orbitals: e (copy), s (swap), 4 single overlaps (Oac,Oad,Obc,Obd), dis (disjoint). Any gadget's port relation is a 7-bit profile. Verified today by exact computation (experiments/combinatorial/w2_parallel_birth.py): FACT A, the 50 stealth profiles (invisible to every width-1 sweep, L45/L56/L57) are closed under MIXED series composition and the generated submonoid contains no forcing element, so chains of any length are dead at width 2, extending L55 past the sibling single-relation result. FACT B, parallel composition (theta-gluing along BOTH ports = intersection of profiles) escapes: the stealth profiles {dis,Oac,Obd,s} and {dis,Oad,Obc,e} meet to {dis}, forcing all four cross pairs different: a virtual K4 on two disjoint unit edges. FACT C, on the plus-frame (two unit edges crossing perpendicularly at their midpoints, vertices (+-1/2,0),(0,+-1/2), cross distances sqrt2/2), D4 acts by plane isometries permuting orbitals; enumeration over all 128 profiles proves: any profile containing dis and missing one overlap bit plus one transporter bit D4-stacks (at most 8 reflected copies) to exactly {dis}.

So the one missing object reduces to: a single 5-colorable plus-framed gadget with TWO stealth missing profile bits. The Euclidean content sits exactly there: a missing bit means a double-merge of the gadget is 6-chromatic, and the stack is a chi=5 clamp, impossible in every control plane; in L^infty, stacking-sufficient deficiency is forbidden outright (else chi(L^infty)>=5), so deficiency is a strictly Euclidean observable.

conjectures.name: Lineage two-point fullness (the deficiency null law)
conjectures.statement: In all 12 chi-5 UDGs of the de Grey/Polymath lineage, every ordered pair of disjoint unit edges (e,f) whose four cross vertex-pairs are non-adjacent has a FULL 7-bit orbital profile: each of the patterns e, s, Oac, Oad, Obc, Obd, dis is realized by some proper 5-coloring. Equivalently, the lineage carries no 2-point color correlations beyond adjacency: in particular every simultaneous double merge (L1=R1 and L2=R2 at once) remains 5-SAT. This is strictly stronger than L57, which tested single merges and single added edges only.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: The profile sweep (7 incremental SAT probes per edge pair, cadical with equality/inequality selector literals) on S199 exhaustively and on the 510-graph high-degree core finds zero missing bits. A single deficient pair refutes it, and any refuting pair is immediately a candidate input to the FACT C stacking machinery (its missing-bit pattern is checked against the cached stacking-sufficient inventory).
conjectures.why_plausible: L57's exhaustive 1-point freeness across ~2.29M pairs shows extreme mixing; the fifth-color slack makes joint patterns generically completable; and the L^infty corollary of FACT C shows chi caps profile behavior, suggesting chi=5 graphs can support at most marginal deficiency. If true it explains structurally why no clamp precursor was ever visible to the existing sweeps: the precursor is a 2-point observable and the lineage has none.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Short-range edge pairs near the spindle cores could block one joint pattern (e.g. the double merge Obd-with-e-blocked) while passing every 1-point test, since L57's tests are blind to correlations. Refutation would be the best possible outcome: the first realizable width-2 deficiency in hand.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Two-point orbital profiles of 5-colorings of de Grey-lineage graphs have never been measured anywhere; L45/L56/L57 (1-point forcing) and Polymath16's k=4 virtual edges are the nearest prior art, both strictly weaker observables.

conjectures.name: Plus-frame stealth deficiency exists (the clamp factory)
conjectures.statement: There exists a finite 5-colorable unit-distance graph G containing the plus-frame (vertices L1=(-1/2,0), L2=(1/2,0), R1=(0,-1/2), R2=(0,1/2); unit edges L1L2 and R1R2 crossing at their midpoints) whose 7-bit profile contains dis, misses at least one overlap bit, and misses at least one transporter bit (e.g. missing exactly {Obd, s}, a stealth pattern invisible to all width-1 sweeps). By the verified FACT C, the D4 stack of at most 8 reflected/rotated copies of G through the shared frame is then a 5-chromatic UDG in which all four cross pairs (distance sqrt2/2, non-adjacent) are forced-different: a unit-distance-realizable clamp, hence chi(R^2) >= 6 via the L24 lift. Equivalently: the W3 missing object is exactly a two-bit-deficient plus-framed gadget, a strictly weaker demand than any previously identified sufficient object (perfect {e,s} pair-transmitters need five missing bits; direct clamps need forcing itself).
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Deficiency-guided breeding: anchor the plus-frame, grow spindle/lattice scaffolding in exact arithmetic over Q(sqrt2, sqrt3, sqrt11), SAT-score each candidate by its number of missing bits over the frame (fitness 0-7, a GRADED objective unlike the binary clamp target L57 proved absent). Prediction: deficiency > 0 appears within ~10^4 candidates; each hit is then one 5-colorability SAT call plus 4 merge-UNSAT probes on its explicit D4 stack. Sustained saturation at deficiency 0 across diverse scaffolds falsifies the conjecture's empirical basis; a hit with a stacking-sufficient pattern decides chi >= 6 outright (subject to bulk-collision recheck).
conjectures.why_plausible: A missing bit requires only a 6-chromatic double-merge minor, the weakest conceivable demand above chi=5 (two merges above a 5-chromatic graph); 6 stealth-sufficient abstract patterns exist (cached inventory); nothing in L45-L57 excludes deficiency since those sweeps measure 1-point observables; and the plus-frame is rational, so only the bulk needs irrational scaffolding. The monoid obstruction (L55, FACT A) is respected: the construction uses parallel, not series, composition.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: A profile-completeness law for 5-colorable UDGs (the width-2 shadow of de Grey's omega<=3 conjecture) would force fullness on all non-adjacent frames, predicting chi=5; or realizable deficiency might exist only on asymmetric frames whose twist subgroups cannot spread transporter bits (V4-frames fix {e,s} setwise, only odd reflections mix them); or bulk vertex collisions in the stack could destroy 5-colorability case by case. Also the stack guarantee needs dis present in the realized profile; a deficient gadget missing dis lands outside FACT C.
conjectures.novelty_claim: The plus-frame, the D4 stacking theorem, the two-missing-bit sufficiency, and the target object 'virtual K4 supported on two disjoint unit edges' are to my knowledge absent from the literature. Nearest prior art: Polymath16 virtual edges (k=4, single pairs), the hat/spectre reflection discourse (reflections for aperiodicity, not color forcing), and this repo's sibling scripts (series-only classification; the {e,s}+K22 closure lemma, which needs a five-bit-deficient transmitter).

conjectures.name: Kempe percolation law for transporter deficiency
conjectures.statement: For a 5-colorable graph with two edge ports, the transporter bit s is missing while e is present if and only if in EVERY proper 5-coloring realizing pattern e, the 2-color Kempe component containing the right port edge (colors c(R1), c(R2)) reaches the left port. The 'only if' direction is a lemma (an e-coloring whose Kempe component avoids the left port swaps into an s-coloring); the conjectural 'if' direction asserts that at k=5 on UDGs, frame-pattern classes of colorings are connected by port-avoiding Kempe moves, so percolation in all e-colorings is the ONLY mechanism that can delete the s bit.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: On small two-ported gadgets (<= 20 vertices, exhaustive coloring enumeration in Python) and on SAT-sampled colorings of larger ones: find a gadget where every e-coloring has a percolating Kempe component yet an s-coloring exists (refutes 'if'), or where s is missing but some e-coloring fails to percolate (refutes the lemma direction, i.e. a bug in the framework). Checkable with networkx connected-components on the 2-color subgraph per coloring; hours at most.
conjectures.why_plausible: Kempe moves plus global recolorings generate most of the 5-coloring space of sparse graphs (Cereceda-van den Heuvel-Johnson-style mixing), so a missing pattern should be attributable to a Kempe disconnection, and percolation between the ports is the unique S_5-invariant disconnector available. It also converts breeding from blind search into mechanism-guided construction: build dense triangulated spindle channels between the two frame edges to force percolation, which is exactly the Euclidean (equilateral-triangle-rich) structure the L^infty detector demands.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Non-Kempe-reachable 5-colorings exist for some graphs (Kempe connectivity can fail below degree thresholds); an s-coloring could then exist in a separate Kempe class with all e-colorings percolating, killing the 'if' direction. The necessary-condition half survives any such counterexample and remains a cheap pre-filter for breeding.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Kempe chains as the exact mechanism characterizing width-2 port deficiency in the Hadwiger-Nelson setting has, to my knowledge, never been stated. Nearest prior art: Kempe-equivalence literature (Mohar, Cereceda et al., Bonamy et al.) and scattered Polymath16 Kempe arguments for 4-colorings; neither connects Kempe percolation to boundary-relation deficiency or to clamp construction.

experiment.description: Phase 0 (DONE, this session): experiments/combinatorial/w2_parallel_birth.py verified FACTS A-C exactly (mixed-series confinement of the 50 stealth profiles; parallel-birth witness {dis,Oac,Obd,s} meet {dis,Oad,Obc,e} = {dis}; D4 stacking theorem over all 128 profiles; 6 stealth stacking-sufficient deficiency patterns, minimum 2 missing bits); cached at experiments/combinatorial/_cache/w2_parallel_birth.json. THE DECISIVE NEXT RUN: width2_profile_sweep.py, the 2-point correlation sweep deciding Conjecture 1 and hunting the Conjecture 2 precursor. For each lineage graph (S199 exhaustively, then the 510-graph high-degree core, reusing load_graph from shotD_exhaustive_forcing.py): enumerate disjoint edge pairs whose four cross pairs are non-adjacent; encode the 5-coloring CNF once per graph with per-pair selector literals (q_uv -> color-equal clauses, r_uv -> color-differ clauses); for each pair run 7 incremental cadical probes, one per orbital pattern (each probe assumes 4 selectors); record the 7-bit profile. Outputs: profile spectrum histogram, every non-full profile with its missing bits checked against the cached stacking-sufficient inventory, and (via sympy exact coordinates) the frame congruence type and symmetry group of any deficient pair, to determine its available twist subgroup. Decision rule: all-full = Conjecture 1 supported, candidate L58 negative sharpening the wall to 2-point correlation freeness and redirecting breeding off-lineage; any deficient pair = first realizable width-2 correlation, immediate input to D4/subgroup stacking and one SAT call from a clamp verdict.
experiment.repo_feasibility: Everything needed exists: python-sat (cadical) with assumption-based incremental solving, networkx for edge enumeration, sympy for exact frame geometry, lineage graph loaders already in experiments/combinatorial/shotD_exhaustive_forcing.py, caching conventions under experiments/combinatorial/_cache/. No C compiler, no new dependencies. Phase 0 script already runs clean in ~30 seconds.
experiment.est_cost: Phase 0: done (30 s). Sweep: S199 full (~10^4-10^5 qualifying edge pairs at 7 sub-second incremental probes each) plus 510-core sample of ~20k pairs: roughly 1-3 hours wall clock, checkpointed like shotD. Script ~250 lines.

detector_self_check.Q2: The method consumes chi=5 raw material and outputs a chi=5 clamp: no graph with chi >= 3 embeds in Q^2 (Woodall, chi=2), so neither the deficient gadget's stack nor the clamp exists there. A missing profile bit itself forces a 6-chromatic double-merge minor, four levels above anything Q^2 supports. FACTS A-C are field-blind pure combinatorics, but they are confinement and transfer statements that prove no bound by themselves; the single bound-producing step (realizing a deficient plus-framed gadget) is exactly W3 realizability, which is the legitimate and explicitly flagged location of all Euclidean content (consistent with L51-L53's relocation).
detector_self_check.Linf: Over-proof is structurally impossible and the check yields a theorem: since every finite L^infty-UDG is 4-colorable, FACT C implies no plus-framed L^infty gadget can carry stacking-sufficient deficiency at k=5 (its stack would be a 5-chromatic L^infty-UDG, contradiction), and no K22-cappable {e,s} transmitter exists there either. So run in L^infty the calculus proves fullness laws, not bounds: deficiency is a strictly Euclidean observable. Independently, the final lift from one forced pair to a forbidden distance requires isometry-group transitivity on equal-length pairs, which fails in L^infty (only 8 preserved directions), and the width-2 rigidity input (two unit circles meet in <= 2 points) fails there (squares meet in segments).
detector_self_check.R1: The line has no chi >= 3 finite unit-distance subgraphs (its unit graph is a union of paths), so the raw material is absent; the plus-frame and its D4 stack use pi/2 rotations and reflections, point-group structure with no 1-dimensional analog; and the argument is combinatorial-geometric, not measure-theoretic, so the rotation-blindness failure mode (a measure argument that would also constrain chi(R)=2) cannot arise: applied to R^1 the calculus asserts only confinement facts and produces no constraint.
